Last updated: 2026-03-10

Best AI Tools for Open House Follow-Up (Convert More Visitors to Clients)

Open house follow-up is one of the most neglected lead conversion opportunities in real estate. AI tools that automate immediate personalized follow-up can triple conversion rates from open house traffic.

4 tools compared7 FAQs answered

Our Recommendation

WinnerFollow Up Boss

Follow Up Boss's open house app and automated follow-up sequences handle the full post-open-house workflow. Capture guest information, automatically segment by buyer vs. neighbor, trigger appropriate follow-up, and track engagement — all from one platform.

Try Follow Up Boss →
Runner-UpStructurely

Structurely's AI text assistant is particularly powerful for open house leads, where the 5-minute window for response is most critical. Visitors who register their phone number and receive a text within minutes of leaving have dramatically higher conversion rates.

Try Structurely →

Not for you if: Agents who do fewer than 4 open houses per month. At low volume, the tools described here are overkill — a simple post-open-house email template and manual follow-up is sufficient.

The average open house attracts 5-15 registered visitors. The average agent follows up with fewer than half of them, and typically with a generic 'Thanks for visiting!' email. AI tools can follow up with 100% of visitors, personalize based on what they said about their situation, and continue nurturing for 12+ months. We tested tools specifically on open house workflows.

Individual Tool Reviews

FU

Follow Up Boss

Editor's Choice

The CRM built specifically for real estate agents

$69/month (solo)

★★★★★4.7/5

Follow Up Boss has earned its market position through a singular focus: making sure no lead falls through the cracks. The platform aggregates leads from every source (Zillow, Realtor.com, website, referrals, manual entry) into one inbox and creates automatic follow-up tasks and reminders. While it's not the most 'AI-heavy' platform, its 2024-2025 AI additions are practical: smart email drafting, lead stage prediction, and automated text responses. What makes Follow Up Boss stand out is its integration ecosystem — it connects to 250+ tools, making it the connective tissue of a real estate tech stack. Acquired by Zillow in 2023 but operating independently.

Pros

  • Best integration ecosystem in real estate CRM
  • Reliable and fast — trusted by 50,000+ real estate professionals
  • Clean UI that agents actually use (adoption is a real issue with CRMs)
  • Excellent mobile app

Cons

  • Less AI-native than competitors like Lofty
  • Predictive analytics less sophisticated than dedicated tools
  • Pricing can add up significantly for large teams

Best for

Serious individual agents and small teams who want a reliable CRM with maximum integration flexibility

Not for

Agents who want a fully autonomous AI system — Follow Up Boss requires agent engagement

Try Follow Up Boss →Learn More
ST

Structurely

Best AI Lead Qualification

AI lead qualification via conversational text

$499/month

★★★★4.4/5

Structurely solves one of the most painful problems in real estate: leads arrive at all hours and go cold within 5 minutes if not contacted. Their AI text assistant engages new leads within seconds, carries on natural-sounding multi-turn conversations to qualify timeline, budget, pre-approval status, and motivation, then books appointments directly into your calendar. The AI handles objections, follow-up attempts, and re-engagement of old leads automatically. In independent testing, Structurely-contacted leads show 2-5x higher conversion rates versus leads that wait for human follow-up. The tool works best when integrated with Follow Up Boss or a similar CRM that feeds leads automatically.

Pros

  • 5-second lead response time — dramatically outperforms human follow-up
  • Qualification quality is genuinely impressive in natural conversation
  • Books appointments autonomously — agents wake up to scheduled showings
  • Re-engages dead leads with surprising success

Cons

  • Expensive for individual agents with low lead volume
  • Occasional conversations that feel slightly robotic to sophisticated buyers
  • Requires CRM integration to work properly — not standalone

Best for

High-volume lead buyers (Zillow Premier Agent, Google Ads) who can't respond to every lead instantly

Not for

Luxury agents with low-volume, high-touch client relationships where AI text feels inappropriate

Try Structurely →Learn More
HB

Homebot

Best for Client Retention

AI home value reports that keep you top of mind with past clients

$25/month

★★★★★4.6/5

Homebot solves the most common problem in real estate: agents spend enormous resources acquiring clients, then lose those relationships between transactions. Homebot sends a personalized monthly report to every homeowner in your database showing their home's current value, equity position, refinance options, and investment return — branded with your name and photo. The reports are genuinely valuable to homeowners (75-85% open rates are typical), creating consistent touchpoints without being intrusive. The platform tracks engagement — when a homeowner starts checking their report frequently, Homebot flags them as a likely mover. Agents typically see 10-20% of their deals annually traced to Homebot engagement.

Pros

  • 75-85% open rates — most-opened email marketing tool in real estate
  • Genuinely valuable to clients — not just advertising
  • Engagement tracking identifies 'hot' clients before they call you
  • Excellent co-marketing with preferred lenders

Cons

  • Reactive — you wait for clients to engage rather than proactively reaching out
  • Requires a meaningful database (100+ contacts) to see ROI
  • Valuation methodology varies from actual appraisal values

Best for

Agents with 100+ past clients or SOI contacts who want automated database maintenance

Not for

New agents with small databases — the math doesn't work with under 50 contacts

Try Homebot →Learn More
ZU

Zurple

AI behavior-based follow-up for real estate leads

$349/month

★★★★4.2/5

Zurple takes a different approach to lead nurturing: instead of fixed email sequences, their AI monitors every property search and view, then sends emails that reference the specific properties the lead is interested in. An email that says 'I noticed you've been looking at 3-bedroom homes in Austin under $500K — here's what just came on the market' performs dramatically better than generic 'Just wanted to check in!' messages. Zurple's behavioral triggers also notify agents when a lead's search activity suggests they're moving toward a decision — increased frequency of searches, revisiting specific properties, changes in search criteria. The tool complements any CRM well and is often used alongside Follow Up Boss.

Pros

  • Behavior-triggered emails dramatically outperform generic sequences
  • Property-specific personalization feels natural, not automated
  • Hot lead alerts catch the decision moment

Cons

  • Requires your leads to come through your IDX website — doesn't work with phone leads
  • Email-focused — no text or calling automation
  • AI isn't as sophisticated as dedicated AI platforms like Structurely

Best for

Agents with strong IDX website traffic who want automated, personalized email follow-up

Not for

Agents whose leads primarily come through referrals, open houses, or non-IDX sources

Try Zurple →Learn More

How to Choose the Right Tool

1
Capture quality matters: AI follow-up is only as good as the information you capture. A digital sign-in (not paper) with name, phone, and email is essential.
2
Speed of first follow-up: Visitors who receive a personalized text within 5 minutes of registering have 3-5x higher response rates. Automation is the only way to achieve this consistently.
3
Segmentation at capture: Not every visitor is a buyer — neighbors, nosy visitors, and sellers checking competition don't need buyer follow-up sequences. Good systems capture intent.
4
Long-term nurture: Most open house visitors aren't ready to buy for 3-6 months. Your follow-up system needs to maintain contact appropriately for the full decision timeline.
5
CRM integration: Open house leads should flow into your main CRM automatically. Manual entry is the biggest failure point.

Frequently Asked Questions

What's the best way to follow up after an open house?
Best practice: Immediate (within 5 min) personalized text from your AI assistant. Within 2 hours, a personal email referencing the property and their stated situation. Same evening or next morning, a brief call from you for anyone who expressed high interest. Within 48 hours, add to appropriate nurture sequence based on their timeline.
How do I capture open house visitor information digitally?
Options range from simple (Google Form on an iPad) to sophisticated (Follow Up Boss's open house app, BombBomb's open house app, or Open Home Pro). Digital capture automatically populates your CRM, eliminates illegible handwriting, and can trigger immediate follow-up. Paper sign-ins should be extinct in a real estate professional's operation.
Should I text or email open house follow-up?
Text first, email follow-up. Open house visitors gave you their phone number — text is appropriate and gets 95%+ read rates vs 25% for email. Keep the text brief and specific to the property. Follow with email for any deeper information. Never use WhatsApp or iMessage for business follow-up — stick to platforms that log to your CRM.
How long should I follow up with open house visitors?
Most open house visitors who convert as clients do so within 3-6 months. However, buyers on longer timelines (12-18 months) represent your future pipeline. A 12-month nurture sequence for all open house visitors who opt in is worth maintaining. Homebot is particularly effective for open house visitors who own homes — they get monthly value on their current home while you stay top of mind.
How do I handle open house visitors who won't give their real contact info?
Focus on conversation quality, not capture quantity. Genuine conversations build rapport that makes visitors want to stay in touch. Offer clear value exchange: 'If you leave your email, I'll send you the home's complete disclosure packet and a list of what's coming on market in this range.' Make the value of sharing information obvious.
Can AI identify which open house visitors are most likely to buy?
Yes. CRMs like Follow Up Boss and Lofty score leads based on engagement signals. In the open house context: visitors who spend extra time, ask specific questions about offer process, mention current lease end dates, or are pre-approved are higher conversion probability. AI can systematically track stated intent and behavioral signals to prioritize your follow-up.
What should I do for the listing agent's open house visitors vs. my buyer clients?
At your own listing, your primary goals are: find a buyer and identify agent referrals. For unrepresented visitors, follow up with buyer representation offers. At other agents' open houses (as a buyer agent), ensure your buyer clients are registered with your information as their agent. Do not attempt to solicit clients from other agents' open houses — it's unethical and violates NAR Code of Ethics.
